seoTitle: How to Execute an n8n Workflow: Test & Run Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to manually trigger, test, and monitor an n8n workflow run, from single node tests to full production execution.
intro: Before trusting an automation to run unattended, it helps to understand the different ways n8n lets you trigger and inspect a workflow. This guide explains how to run a manual test, check individual node outputs for errors, and move a workflow from testing into live production mode.

steps.heading: Open your target workflow
steps.body: Load the automation project canvas you want to execute from your n8n dashboard.

steps.heading: Trigger a manual execution
steps.body: Use the Test Workflow button at the bottom of the canvas to run the entire node sequence at once.

steps.heading: Watch the live status indicators
steps.body: Green checkmarks show successful steps while red alerts flag errors, so you can quickly spot where something broke.

steps.heading: Inspect input and output data
steps.body: Open a node's preview tab to see the exact JSON data it received and produced, which is the fastest way to debug mismatched fields.

steps.heading: Switch on production triggers
steps.body: Once you're confident in the results, activate the workflow so webhooks or scheduled triggers run automatically in the background.

steps.heading: Review the execution history
steps.body: Check the Executions tab periodically to confirm your automation keeps running correctly over time.

faq.question: What's the difference between testing a single node and running the full workflow?
faq.answer: Testing a single node runs just that step in isolation, while executing the full workflow processes data sequentially through every connected node.

faq.question: Can I see what data was passed between nodes after a run?
faq.answer: Yes, each node's input/output preview tab lets you inspect the exact JSON payload at that step, which is useful for tracking down bottlenecks.

faq.question: Do I have to manually run my workflow every time?
faq.answer: No, once you've tested it you can activate scheduled or webhook triggers so it runs automatically without manual intervention.

faq.question: Where can I see past runs of my workflow?
faq.answer: The Executions tab keeps a history log of past runs so you can audit results and catch failures after the fact.
